name: research
description: v2.0.0 - Agent-driven R&D redesign. Frascati 3 categories, Toulmin-derived claim structure, analysis discipline, Z39.18-style human-readable reports. Breaking change from v1.x.

interface.shortDescription: Agent-driven R&D protocol for basic/applied/development research with claim and analysis discipline
interface.longDescription: v2.0.0 - A protocol skill for agent-driven R&D across the three Frascati categories: basic research (understanding phenomena, building baselines), applied research (achieving measurable objectives), and experimental development (building working systems). Enforces shared vocabulary, Toulmin-derived claim structure with explicit alternatives and conditions, analysis discipline (EDA + result analysis + depth stop conditions), and human-readable reports with required figures. Includes a quant-research extension for time-series and statistically rigorous quantitative R&D (purged CV, multiple testing, leakage detection). Methods reproducibility is enforced; experiment-level replicability (env locks, commit pins) is the agent's discretion. Breaking change from v1.x.
defaultPrompt: Design an R&D plan for this investigation
Help me decide what to do after this experimental result
Write a human-readable report from this plan and runs
